ac_validation.text: Given the mixins are defined, when a plugin class inherits from PluginBase and FileProvider, then it must implement only name, display_name, description, files(), and directories() — no forced empty methods.
issues: The phrase 'no forced empty methods' is a design description (ISP compliance), not a test assertion. The testable interpretation is: 'a PluginBase + FileProvider subclass can be instantiated without implementing methods from CommandRunner, DependencyProvider, or Configurable.' This is testable via isinstance checks and successful instantiation.

The AC does not explicitly verify that requires and run_after (inherited from PluginBase) are accessible with their default values. This would be a useful additional assertion to confirm the full interface surface.
ac_validation.suggested_fix: Reword to: 'Given PluginBase and FileProvider are defined, when a class inherits from both, then it must instantiate successfully by implementing only name, display_name, description, files(), and directories(), and isinstance checks for uninherited mixins (CommandRunner, Configurable, DependencyProvider) must return False.'

ac_validation.text: Given a plugin that does NOT inherit CommandRunner, when isinstance(plugin, CommandRunner) is checked, then it returns False.
Given PluginBase is abstract, when instantiated directly, then TypeError is raised.
Given the module, when searched for imports from ui, generation, or infrastructure, then none exist (may import from domain only).
issues: The AC must scan BOTH files: src/forge/plugins/base.py and src/forge/plugins/__init__.py. The AST scanner from TestAC7_NoCrossLayerImports must be parameterized to point at the plugins/ directory.

The AC phrasing 'may import from domain only' is slightly too restrictive — stdlib imports (abc, pathlib, typing) are also needed. The intent is clearly that within forge-internal imports, only domain is allowed. The test should permit stdlib imports while forbidding forge.ui, forge.generation, forge.infrastructure.

The API spec code block shows from abc import ABC, abstractmethod but does NOT show imports for Question, ProjectSpec, GeneratedFile, Path — these types appear in method signatures and must be imported from forge.domain or stdlib. This is a doc completeness issue, not a testability blocker.
ac_validation.suggested_fix: Clarify AC text: '...when searched for imports from forge.ui, forge.generation, or forge.infrastructure, then none exist. Imports from forge.domain and stdlib are permitted.'

infrastructure_readiness.notes: Must be created. Follow class-per-AC pattern from test_domain_models.py. AC-004 AST scanner requires parameterization to scan plugins/ directory instead of domain/.

EXISTS with 5 mock plugin classes (FileOnlyPlugin, ConfigOnlyPlugin, CommandOnlyPlugin, DependencyOnlyPlugin, FullPlugin) and corresponding fixtures. KNOWN ISSUE: module-level imports from forge.plugins.base will cause ModuleNotFoundError at collection time until base.py is implemented. This is explicitly non-blocking per instructions.

All domain models needed by mixin signatures exist and are properly exported via domain/__init__.py. Question uses correct field name question_type: QuestionType. ProjectSpec, GeneratedFile, QuestionType all confirmed importable.

infrastructure_readiness.requirement: AST scanner pattern for architecture enforcement
infrastructure_readiness.notes: The ast.parse() pattern is well-established. For AC-004, the scanner must be parameterized to: (1) scan plugins/ instead of domain/, (2) allow forge.domain and stdlib imports, (3) forbid forge.ui, forge.generation, forge.infrastructure. The existing FORBIDDEN_PREFIXES tuple can be reused with 'forge.plugins' removed from the forbidden list.
Convention is established in all existing source and test files. conftest.py already uses it. test_plugin_base.py should follow the same pattern.
